examples.title: Factorial using Recursion (proved by induction)
examples.description: Recursive factorial function - correctness proven by induction on n
examples.explanation: The correctness of this recursive algorithm is proven by mathematical induction. The base case returns correct value, and assuming it works for k, it correctly computes for k+1.

examples.title: Sum of First N Natural Numbers
examples.description: Algorithm to compute sum 1+2+...+n with proof by induction
examples.code:
FUNCTION sum_n(n):
 IF n == 0 THEN
 RETURN 0
 ELSE
 RETURN n + sum_n(n-1)
 END IF
END FUNCTION

// OR iterative version:
FUNCTION sum_iterative(n):
 sum = 0
 FOR i FROM 1 TO n:
 sum = sum + i
 RETURN sum
END FUNCTION

// Formula: sum_n(n) = n(n+1)/2
// Proven by: Base case n=1: 1 = 1(2)/2
// Inductive step: assume sum_n(k) = k(k+1)/2
// sum_n(k+1) = (k+1) + k(k+1)/2 = (k+1)(k+2)/2
examples.explanation: Both recursive and iterative versions compute the same result. The formula n(n+1)/2 can be proven by induction and gives O(1) solution.

examples.title: Strong Induction - Fibonacci Properties
examples.description: Prove Fibonacci property using strong induction
examples.explanation: Cassini's identity for Fibonacci numbers is proven using strong induction as it requires two base cases and uses earlier values in the inductive step.

examples.title: Proof by Induction - Power Rule
examples.explanation: This proof demonstrates the exponentiation rule using induction. The key step is using the inductive hypothesis to transform (ab)ᵏ into aᵏbᵏ and then multiplying by ab.
